text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously optimistic - small caps are having a banner year (headline #5), AI export restrictions are easing (Anthropic/Claude), and macro noise around Trump's financial disclosures is background static. Your names - chips, tech, semis - are sensitive to AI narrative and export policy, both of which are tilting positive today.

Your holdings: Everything is green and stops are intact - no immediate fires. That said, SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged semi ETF that decays and swings violently; on margin that's a double amplifier, so your $200 stop deserves a hard look if semis wobble. SPAL is brand-new, very volatile, and leveraged - treat it as a small, tight-leash position; $21.38 stop is your line in the sand. QQQI (+0.67%) is barely moving - fine as income anchor, nothing urgent.

Watch: 1) Any follow-through in semi names off AI export news - SMH and SOXL are your direct reads. 2) Whether IWM/small-cap strength continues; your QQQ/SPY broad exposure benefits if risk appetite holds.

Bottom line: Book is healthy - protect gains on your leveraged names and don't add size until a clean setup with a real catalyst appears.
plays: SOXL: Your stop is $200 - honor it hard given 3x leverage on margin. No reason to add here without a pullback to a tighter entry.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, very volatile. Keep size small, stop at $21.38 is your exit - no adjustments without a clear reason.
No new buys today - scanner shows ALAB as the cleanest setup (9/12, entry $481.96-482.54, stop $479.73) but no hard catalyst in today's headlines to pull the trigger; watch it.
